name: Angada
description: Angada is a prominent figure in the Hindu epic Ramayana. He is the son of Vali, the mighty monkey king, and Tara. Born with extraordinary strength and intelligence, Angada played a crucial role in Rama's quest to rescue Sita from Ravana. Following Vali's death at the hands of Rama, Angada initially struggled with feelings of resentment and loss. However, he was eventually taken under Rama's wing and became a key member of the Vanara army. He possessed remarkable diplomatic skills, courage, and unwavering loyalty to Rama. One of his most significant contributions was his mission to Ravana's court as Rama's emissary, where he fearlessly delivered Rama's ultimatum and even managed to unseat the powerful Ravana from his throne with a single stomp, showcasing his immense strength and bravery. Despite facing challenges and internal conflicts, Angada ultimately proved to be an invaluable asset to Rama's cause, contributing significantly to the victory over Ravana and the restoration of dharma. He is revered for his wisdom, strength, and unwavering devotion.
subtitle: Vali's son, Rama's loyal emissary.
achievements: Served as Rama's emissary to Ravana.
Unseated Ravana from his throne in Lanka.
Played a crucial role in the Vanara army.
Helped build the bridge to Lanka.

keyMyths.title: Angada as Emissary
keyMyths.description: Angada bravely went to Ravana's court as Rama's messenger, delivering a stern warning. He demonstrated his immense strength by unseating Ravana from his throne.

keyMyths.title: Angada's Lineage
keyMyths.description: As the son of Vali, Angada inherited immense strength and faced challenges after his father's death. He eventually found purpose serving Rama.

keyMyths.title: Angada's Loyalty
keyMyths.description: Angada demonstrated unwavering loyalty to Rama throughout the war against Ravana. He was a key strategist and a valuable asset to the Vanara army.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: The primary source for Angada's story and deeds.
sources.text: The primary source for Angada's story and deeds.

sources.author: Tulsidas
sources.description: A popular retelling of the Ramayana, featuring Angada.
